title: crepis froelichiana subsp. dinarica
intro: Crepis froelichiana subsp. dinarica, also known as a rare subspecies of the creeping hawk's-beard, is a plant species in the dandelion family. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Crepis froelichiana subsp. dinarica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
